title: The Techno Chronicles
subtitle: A scroll-telling experience documenting the history of techno.

slides.title: The Techno Chronicles
slides.description: No brief, no client, no user research — so I gave myself one constraint: make Wikipedia's techno content feel like the music sounds. Built solo in Figma and Webflow.

headlineMetric.label: Live on Webflow
headlineMetric.context: Self-initiated · Solo designer + developer · Figma + Webflow

slides.label: The Brief
slides.title: Wikipedia's layout didn't fit the story
slides.content: Wikipedia covers the history. None of it feels like the music. My rule: same source, make it feel like what it describes.
slides.highlight: Not a redesign of Wikipedia — a reimagining of how its content could feel.
issues: Dense text walls, no visual hierarchy — information, not experience
Generic sidebar makes every topic feel identical
No pacing — every paragraph competes for attention

slides.label: Ideation
slides.title: Directions I explored
slides.dir1Desc: Text-forward redesign — clean type over Wikipedia's layout. It read like a design exercise, not a cultural experience.
slides.dir2Desc: Visual maximalism — photography, gradients, full-bleed texture. The decoration competed with the story instead of carrying it.
slides.dir3Desc: Rhythmic restraint — monochrome grain, ASCII portraits, scroll-triggered reveals. The look captures the underground feel without spelling it out. Works across every screen size.

slides.title: Where information becomes atmosphere
slides.label: Final Design
slides.description: Monochrome grain, ASCII silhouettes, oversized type — every visual choice earned its place. Nothing made it in unless the content demanded it.
slides.caption: The Techno Chronicles — live on Webflow

slides.label: Reflection
slides.title: What I'd do differently
whatWorked: The monochrome-grain system held the aesthetic together across every section — one committed rule, no style guide needed.
whatFailed: I shipped on gut feeling — no scroll-speed testing, no reader feedback before launch.
whatYoudDoDifferently: Test three scroll speeds with five readers before going live — pacing is the hardest thing to judge alone.
slides.whatYouLearned: Controlling where attention lands — through scroll timing or through a multi-step workflow — is the same design problem. That insight transfers.
slides.whatYouCouldntMeasure: Whether someone unfamiliar with techno still feels the genre's energy by the end — the whole point, and I never measured it.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: Designing experiences that move with the content.
